response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist software engineer with a strong web and full-stack background, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise. The resume shows no SoC or IP modeling, no GPU or AI accelerator work, no deep-learning workload analysis, and no hardware-software co-design experience. Although he meets the years-of-experience bar (BS plus 14+ years) and has some low-latency systems work in DLang and C/C++, the core required skill set is almost entirely absent, making this a fundamental domain mismatch rather than a stretch fit.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, desktop, and systems domains
Genuine low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong open-source contributions, teaching, and knowledge-sharing track record
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is senior full-stack product engineering, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU performance expertise
Solid general systems and low-latency programming foundation
